name: GHR deficiency cohort had low cancer, diabetes, insulin, and pro-aging signaling
summary: The Ecuadorian GHRD cohort is the clearest human natural-experiment anchor for low GH/IGF signaling and disease-protection hypotheses.
statement: In a 22-year Ecuadorian growth-hormone-receptor-deficiency cohort, affected individuals had severe IGF-1 deficiency, lower insulin and HOMA-IR, one nonlethal malignancy, and no diabetes, while unaffected relatives had higher cancer and diabetes prevalence.
population_or_model: Ecuadorian individuals with severe growth hormone receptor deficiency and unaffected relatives
quantitative_note: The report cites one nonlethal malignancy and no diabetes among affected individuals versus 17% cancer and 5% diabetes in controls, with insulin 1.4 uU/ml versus 4.4 and HOMA-IR 0.34 versus 0.96.
caveats: This is a developmental genetic natural experiment, not an adult-onset intervention.
Severe lifelong GHR deficiency is not equivalent to a practical preventive therapy.
The cohort is rare and should not be treated as direct proof that intentional GH/IGF attenuation improves healthy lifespan.
